quotes.text: It's no consolation to you of course, but the 'best' AI generated writing (IMO) will always be a.) inferior to exceptional human literary writing and b.) always generic enough to instantly recognize as the creation of algorythms. The kind of anodyne, insipid garbage that CoPilot pumps out when you give it a story prompt has the same hackneyed tropes and cliches from the pulp fiction it's raided for inspiration ('Once upon a time/the air crackled in anticipation etc.). It's also noticeable in the digital trash that Yahoo et al pump out in their AI generated news copy has zero awareness of register or target reader (using words such as 'cops'/kids etc. Unfortunately, AI's adoption will thrive because of the greed of corporations (who couldn't give a fuck about cultural and civilizational impact) and the greed and myopia of universities who will allow sts. to use this scam technology to write their essays and do the required course reading (Summarizers - they save you time eh and will destroy your literacy skills, great eh?). Don't get me started on how criminals are going to exploit AI. As a character in a William S Burroughs novel once said: 'It's a sick picture,'

quotes.text:
I found your video on the best ChatGPT copywriting prompt to be incredibly informative and helpful. Your detailed explanation of the components of an effective prompt and the examples you provided, such as Facebook ad copy and sales page copy, really helped me understand how to craft persuasive copy using ChatGPT. I appreciate the encouragement to try the prompt and the call to action to join your community for more copywriting resources.
I have been experimenting with ChatGPT for copywriting and have seen significant improvements in my work. Your video has given me even more confidence in using this technology to streamline my workflow and focus on core research and analysis. I agree that it is essential to review and adapt ChatGPT outputs carefully to ensure accuracy and originality.
Thank you for sharing your expertise and insights on using ChatGPT for copywriting. I look forward to exploring more of your content and learning from your experiences.
quotes.author: @SOURCE_ENLIGHTENMENT101

quotes.text: Hi @AndreClarkeRemote, thanks for your feedback. This is the best ChatGPT  copywriting prompt, not one for databases.
quotes.author: @JesseForrest

quotes.text: I recently found you and saw you've been selling AI copywriting software. I was interested in what you would say about ChatGPT but you didn't even mention it (besides saying prompts won't work) or explain the difference of your product compared to ChatGPT. Also for someone new to your products I haven't heard of the other two you mentioned working with your software. It would have been nice to hear more about what they are what they do and how they work together.
quotes.author: @anthonyrude

quotes.text:
my predictions for what will emerge:
The "Human Authenticity" Premium & The Rise of "Artisanal" Marketing:
New Roles: "Brand Authenticity Guardians," storytellers who can weave genuinely personal narratives.

The AI "Orchestrator" or "Prompter-Strategist":
.New Skills: Deep understanding of AI capabilities, creative prompting, iterative refinement, data analysis to optimize AI performance.

Hyper-Personalization Becomes the Default, Demanding Deeper Strategy:
New Focus: Deep customer journey mapping, psychological profiling (ethically), advanced segmentation strategy.

The "Post-Content" Experience Economy:
New Focus: Community management, experiential marketing, interactive content formats (beyond text).


Niche and Boutique Agencies Focusing on "Anti-AI" Strengths:
Prediction: While large agencies adopt AI for scale and efficiency, smaller, boutique agencies will thrive by specializing in areas where AI is weak: deep industry-specific nuance, highly original conceptual work, complex emotional storytelling, and building genuine C-suite level trust.
Emergence: These agencies will emphasize their human talent, unique methodologies, and ability to produce work that is demonstrably impossible for an AI to generate.
quotes.author: @SystemThinkers
